content: Orphan drug designation process: sponsors submit request to FDA's Office of Orphan Products Development (OOPD) before NDA submission, providing prevalence data, scientific rationale, and preliminary evidence. Designation can be granted at any development stage but provides no guarantee of approval. Benefits include: 25% tax credit on qualified clinical testing expenses, annual grant funding up to $200,000 for protocol development, written assistance from FDA on study design, and 7-year exclusivity starting at approval date. Exclusivity prevents FDA from approving same drug for same indication regardless of patent status, with exceptions for clinical superiority or insufficient supply. Some drugs receive multiple orphan designations for different rare diseases.
